_meta: Per-element two-axis assessments from visual inspection of the artwork this session. Roles/statuses use two-axis-model.md. This is analysis for review, not a fixture change.

elements.rationale: prominent header, but a vineyard-block/bottling designation, not the house brand
origin (§4.34)
appears only in the URL and 'PRODUCED & BOTTLED BY' line; no separate marketed brand shown, so §4.33(a) deems the responsible person the brand

patricia-green-cellars.artworkSupports: Patricia Green Cellars as the responsible person; a block-designation as the prominent text
patricia-green-cellars.artworkCannotEstablish: whether the wine is marketed under a separate brand not shown on this back panel
patricia-green-cellars.notes: Textbook §4.33(a) fallback. genuinelyAmbiguous=true is appropriate. Two-axis representation would record it as RESPONSIBLE_PERSON + FALLBACK_DEEMED_BRAND rather than a bare OBSERVED value.

elements.rationale: grape type (§4.34)
presented as a vineyard designation directly under the varietal; could be the marketed brand OR a single-vineyard designation
'PRODUCED & BOTTLED BY DAMIANI WINE CELLARS'; a Wine Cellars responsible person, correctly forbidden as brand here

approved-wine-049.artworkSupports: Damiani Wine Cellars as responsible person; Caywood Vineyard as a vineyard name
approved-wine-049.artworkCannotEstablish: whether 'Caywood Vineyard' is the marketed brand or a single-vineyard designation of the Damiani brand
approved-wine-049.notes: The corpus applies rule B correctly (responsible person forbidden) but the brand truth 'Caywood Vineyard' may itself be a vineyard-designation rather than a brand. Needs Joseph.

elements.rationale: large stylized header identity; also the 'bottled by' name — brand and responsible person are the same entity (§24.257 'if different')
grape type (§4.34), correctly forbidden

approved-wine-052.artworkSupports: Mountain Valley Winery as the marketed brand and the bottler
approved-wine-052.notes: Clean explicit brand that is also the responsible person. Company designator 'winery' present but the name is genuinely the marketed identity, not merely a bottler line.

elements.rationale: prominent gold-boxed front identity
secondary fanciful bottling name under the brand; forbidden
'Produced and bottled by Round Peak Vineyards ... for Golden Road Vineyards' — the bottler is a DIFFERENT vineyard than the brand

approved-wine-053.artworkSupports: Golden Road Vineyards as brand; Round Peak Vineyards as the (different) responsible person
approved-wine-053.notes: Two 'Vineyards' names, only one is the brand. The 'for Golden Road Vineyards' trade-name phrase distinguishes them. Strong support for rule B.

elements.rationale: prominent header identity; also 'Bottled By 3 Steves Winery' — brand = responsible person

three-steves-winery.artworkSupports: 3 Steves Winery as marketed brand and bottler
three-steves-winery.notes: One of four current OBSERVED cases. Winery name IS the marketed brand (prominent header), not merely a bottler.

elements.rationale: prominent top-panel identity with its own address block
'PRODUCED AND BOTTLED BY HINNANT FARMS VINEYARD' — a DIFFERENT entity than the brand; forbidden

approved-wine-073.artworkSupports: Mike's Farm, Inc. as the marketed brand; Hinnant as a separate responsible person
approved-wine-073.notes: Explicit company brand with a SEPARATE responsible person. One of four OBSERVED. Not a fallback case: the brand and responsible person are different entities.

elements.rationale: prominent top-panel identity with its own address block, same layout as approved-wine-073
'PRODUCED AND BOTTLED BY HINNANT VINEYARDS' — a different responsible person; forbidden

approved-wine-074.artworkSupports: Mike's Farm, Inc. as marketed brand; Hinnant as separate responsible person
approved-wine-074.notes: SPECIAL CASE. Corrects the region-round hypothesis of 'presence: absent'. 'Mike's Farm, Inc.' is displayed as the brand identity (its own panel), NOT merely a bottler line, and the bottler is a different entity (Hinnant). Current truth 'Mike's Farm' is DEFENSIBLE. The earlier 'no other brand-like text -> absent' reasoning was wrong: the company name is the explicit brand here. Do NOT redirect to Hinnant. Review recommended only to confirm this reversal.

elements.rationale: green script top mark; could be the marketed brand OR a company/responsible-person name
large dark-red script center mark, at least as prominent as Barn Sill; a fanciful/product name that may be the marketed brand
kind-of-wine designation (muscadine); correctly forbidden

approved-wine-083.artworkSupports: two competing brand-candidate marks (Barn Sill Wine Co. and Christmas Hayride) plus a class/type line
approved-wine-083.artworkCannotEstablish: which of Barn Sill Wine Co. and Christmas Hayride is the marketed brand vs a product/series name
approved-wine-083.notes: SPECIAL CASE 2. The prior decision forbids BOTH Christmas Hayride and NC Muscadine Wine, keeping Barn Sill Wine Co. The artwork alone does not resolve Barn Sill (company) vs Christmas Hayride (fanciful) as the brand. Genuinely UNRESOLVED_FROM_ARTWORK; the forbidden-Christmas-Hayride decision may be too strong. Needs Joseph + possibly permit/COLA facts.

elements.rationale: prominent header identity; same brand family as three-steves-winery
type + source-vineyard reference in prose

approved-wine-086.notes: One of four OBSERVED. forbidden 'Curious' looks like a cross-label guard; not central.

elements.rationale: large stylized house mark with registered-trademark symbol; also 'PRODUCED & BOTTLED BY M CELLARS' and MCELLARS.COM
proprietor names, not the brand

m-cellars-baseline.artworkSupports: M Cellars as the marketed brand and bottler
m-cellars-baseline.artworkCannotEstablish: whether the registered mark is 'M Cellars' or the standalone glyph 'M'
m-cellars-baseline.notes: genuinelyAmbiguous=true likely because the dominant glyph is a stylized 'M'. Truth 'M Cellars' defensible. Review only to confirm the mark wording.

elements.rationale: front-panel script identity, repeated as the back-panel header
grape type; correctly forbidden
source/producer vineyard named on the front

wine-multi-artifact-04.artworkSupports: Dry Cellar as marketed brand; a varietal and a source vineyard as non-brand
wine-multi-artifact-04.notes: The reference case for the varietal-vs-brand principle applied to wine-multi-artifact-07.

elements.rationale: front-panel orange display identity
'Scuppernong' is a muscadine grape cultivar and 'White' a colour; a varietal/type designation, structurally parallel to 'Sauvignon Blanc' on wine-multi-artifact-04
back-panel responsible-person restatement of the same brand entity

wine-multi-artifact-07.artworkSupports: Mike's Farm as marketed brand; Scuppernong White as varietal/type
wine-multi-artifact-07.artworkCannotEstablish: n/a beyond the varietal reading being a judgment
wine-multi-artifact-07.notes: SPECIAL CASE 3. Apply the wine-multi-artifact-04 principle: 'Scuppernong White' is varietal/type, not the brand. Current truth 'Mike's Farm' is DEFENSIBLE. Review recommended only to confirm consistency with wma-04.

elements.rationale: large front header identity; also 'PRODUCED AND BOTTLED BY DUCK WALK VINEYARDS'
grape type; correctly forbidden

wine-multi-artifact-09.artworkSupports: Duck Walk Vineyards as marketed brand and bottler
wine-multi-artifact-09.notes: One of four OBSERVED. Vineyards name IS the marketed brand (prominent header).

approved-wine-046.artworkSupports: a non-company fanciful brand with a separate producer name
approved-wine-046.notes: Control: clear non-company marketed brand + separate name/address.

approved-wine-022.artworkSupports: no clear marketed brand on the shown panel
approved-wine-022.artworkCannotEstablish: whether a brand exists on another panel; whether Marble Creek Acres is the responsible person (and thus a fallback brand)
approved-wine-022.notes: Control: brand-absent. Raises the same fallback question as unresolved-evidence.md — is a forbidden name actually the §4.33(a) fallback brand?

elements.rationale: appears here as a sourced-from / producer reference, NOT as the brand identity of THIS product

approved-wine-082.artworkSupports: 3 Steves Winery mentioned but not as this product's brand
approved-wine-082.artworkCannotEstablish: this product's actual brand from the shown panel
approved-wine-082.notes: KEY CONTROL: the same 'winery' name that IS the brand on 060/086 is here NOT the brand. Proves brand status is contextual, not lexical.

approved-wine-091.notes: Control: imported, importer + foreign producer visible.
